vBRIEFInfo.description: Bug in skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 3 (Update Master) and #727 orchestrator role-separation guidance: post-merge cleanup implicitly assumes the merging agent owns the worktree where `git pull origin <base>` runs. In multi-worktree swarm patterns, this lets the merger detach HEAD on a sibling worktree another agent is actively using.

plan.title: swarm SKILL: prohibit `git checkout` in a worktree another agent owns (post-merge cleanup overreach)

narratives.Problem: Phase 6 Step 3 of the swarm SKILL describes post-merge `git pull origin <base-branch>` without scoping to the merger's own worktree. In multi-worktree patterns (the documented multi-agent pattern from AGENTS.md), the merging agent can `cd` into a sibling worktree another agent owns and run `git checkout master` to perform the pull -- detaching HEAD on the other agent's branch. Recurrence record: PR #797 merge session (2026-05-01) -- Agent A owned C:\repos\Deft\directive on a refinement branch; Agent B (merger) owned a sibling worktree; Agent B ran `cd C:\repos\Deft\directive; git checkout master --quiet` after merging its own PR. No work lost (Agent A had pushed) but recovery was retroactive, not preventative.
narratives.Overview: Add a `⊗` MUST NOT rule to Phase 6 Step 3 prohibiting `git checkout` (any branch) in a worktree the merger does not own, plus a companion `!` MUST rule that the merger MAY remove its OWN worktree and orphaned local feature branch but MUST NOT alter any other worktree's HEAD or branch state. Post-merge state-update is performed via `git fetch origin <base>` from the merger's own worktree, OR left to the human operator.
narratives.Background: Multi-agent worktree patterns are explicitly documented in AGENTS.md ('another agent is working in branch ...' rule the user cited at PR #797 kickoff). The current Phase 6 Step 3 ruleset predates the multi-worktree adoption and implicitly assumes single-worktree. Companion to #727 (Sub-Agent Role Separation) -- this work extends the same boundary discipline from sub-agent spawn shape to worktree HEAD operations.
narratives.Constraint: MUST be skill prose addition (no code). MUST land in Phase 6 Step 3 (Update Master). MUST cite #727 as the companion role-separation rule and PR #797 as the recurrence record. MUST NOT weaken the existing Phase 6 Step 3 master-update guidance for the merger's own worktree (still valid). SHOULD use `git fetch origin <base>` as the canonical replacement for `git pull` when the merger needs the latest base-branch state for follow-up decisions.
narratives.Risk: Very low. Skill prose change only; no code. The existing single-worktree pattern continues to work because the merger's OWN worktree is unaffected. The new rules are net additions, not subtractions.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 3 carries the new `⊗` rule (no checkout in another worktree) and the companion `!` rule (merger MAY remove its own worktree + orphaned branch; MUST NOT touch others). (2) Anti-Patterns block gains a corresponding bullet citing PR #797 as recurrence record. (3) Cross-reference to #727 Sub-Agent Role Separation present. (4) Content test asserts the new rule tokens. (5) CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased] / Fixed.
narratives.Test: tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py (existing OR new): regex-assert Phase 6 Step 3 contains the canonical `⊗` rule tokens and the `!` companion rule tokens. Failure messages cite file + missing pattern. Mirror the pattern in tests/content/test_skills.py for Sub-Agent Role Separation tokens.
narratives.Action: 1. Edit skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 3 with the verbatim text from the issue body's 'Proposed rule' block. 2. Add a corresponding bullet to the Anti-Patterns block citing PR #797 as recurrence record. 3. Add/extend tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py with regex assertions on the new rule tokens. 4. CHANGELOG [Unreleased] / Fixed entry. 5. task check; commit; push; PR.
narratives.Urgency: Low-medium severity per the issue. No work lost in the recurrence; risk is higher if a future agent's working tree is dirty when the same overreach happens. Cheap to land now while the cohort is open; small skill-prose change with no design risk.

items.title: Add `⊗` no-checkout-in-others-worktree rule to Phase 6 Step 3

narrative.Action: Insert verbatim per the issue body's 'Proposed rule' block. Anchor: skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 6 Step 3 (Update Master).
Companion rule clarifying what the merger IS allowed to do (remove own worktree + orphaned branch) so the `⊗` is not read as 'merger does nothing post-merge'.

items.title: Add Anti-Patterns block bullet citing PR #797 as recurrence record

narrative.Action: Mirror the existing Sub-Agent Role Separation anti-pattern shape (#727). Cite the 2026-05-01 merge session and the concrete `cd ...; git checkout master --quiet` pattern that triggered it.
Regex-assert Phase 6 Step 3 contains the canonical rule tokens. Test failure message: file + missing pattern.
fix(skills/swarm): prohibit git checkout in another agent's worktree during post-merge cleanup; add companion merger-may-remove-own rule and anti-pattern citing PR #797 (#800).

items.title: Run task check and confirm clean

narrative.Action: Pre-PR gate. Content test must pass.
